translated: Love Live! Superstar!! Yuigaoka Broadcasting Station, Liella!
This is the Yuigaoka Girls' High School Suggestion Box, where we introduce your messages.
Alright everyone, let's say it together! One, two... Suggestion Box!
First, we have one from Liella name: Miku.

Thank you! They say, "I attended both days of the 7th Live in Kanagawa and watched the Aichi show via stream. Mei-chan's solo song, 'Sky Linker,' started by taking the baton from the club president, Chisato-chan."

"What was especially striking was the part at the end of the chorus where you thrust your fist forward. Since it happened several times, I was so moved I almost cried the first time, and from the second time on, I was thrusting my fist along with you."
"It was a performance where I felt the full extent of Mei-chan's straightforward strength, including her big heart, the kindness that embraces weakness, and her desire to protect."
Next is from Liella name: Baron Ojipan.
"Regarding the impressions of 'Sky Linker,' the clapping with your hands near your face..."

"...and the side steps that followed looked like Flamenco, a traditional Spanish dance. Spain is often called the land of passion, and I felt you expressed Mei-chan's hidden passion through the dance. The moment I saw it, I thought, 'Whoa, that's so cool!'"
Alright, next is Liella name: Red-haired Kana. Thank you!

"Seeing 'Sky Linker' on Day 1 in Kanagawa was truly the best. Yabu-chan's dance was so cool I can't get it out of my head. The song and lyrics are great, so I listen to it constantly. I'd love to see the dance for 'Sky Linker' again somewhere!"
I want to hear more about this.
'Sky Linker' was so cool. At first, I thought the performance might be done with a stand mic, just standing there and hyping up the crowd.
I couldn't really imagine you doing an intense dance to it.
I kept wondering how it would turn out, but I never expected Flamenco in the instrumental break. It was a really valuable experience, though. You don't usually get to do Flamenco.
When I first saw the video of Mei-chan's choreography...
I was like, "What is this dance?!"
Mei-chan was doing a style of dance that none of us had ever done before.
I said, "Oh, this is amazing!" and when I heard it was Flamenco, I was so surprised.
Yeah, I was like, "How do you even do this?"
I couldn't do it at all at first.
It used a core strength I'd never used before, and the whole foundation of the movement...
How do I put it? The basics were totally different. I'm not an expert on dance or anything,
but even so, it was so difficult I didn't understand it. I practiced just that instrumental part at home over and over.
But you were so good at it!

It was incredibly cool. Since we all do our solo choreography lessons separately, we don't know when others are practicing or even if their songs involve dancing. When I'd see other members, I'd ask, "Wait, are you dancing to Kanon-chan's song?"
We had those kinds of conversations a lot.
I happened to run into Yabu right after her choreography lesson,
and she muttered something like, "I think it was Flamenco..."
And I was like, "Wait, what do you mean?" I was so confused for a second.
Like Yabu said earlier, I expected her to just stand tall with a stand mic,
with a sort of burning intensity. So when I heard Flamenco, I was like...
I mean, you don't really get many chances to see Flamenco, right?
Liella! probably wouldn't do it normally.
Not all of us together. It must be hard when you don't even know what the "correct" way looks like.
It looks difficult just watching it, so I think it would've been a huge struggle if I had to do it. But I saw how hard you were practicing.
Yeah, but your movements were so sharp!
It was so cool. And like Miku-san said, that part where you thrust your fist forward felt so straightforward and very much like Mei-chan.
